[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 賴主翁善良 - Thanks to the kind host; '賴' means 'thanks to' or 'relying on,' and '主翁' refers to the host or master.
  • 易以裳衣 - Exchanged his clothes; '易' means 'to exchange' or 'to replace.'
  • 待以酒食 - Treated with food and drink; '待' means 'to treat' or 'to provide.'
  • 贈以盤纏 - Gave him money for travel; '贈' means 'to give' and '盤纏' refers to travel expenses or money.
  • 遣之曰 - Sent him off with these words; '遣' means 'to send away' or 'to dismiss.'
  • 既遭寇劫 - After being robbed by bandits; '寇劫' refers to robbery or plunder.
  • 理合聞官 - According to the law, one should report to the authorities; '理合' means 'according to the law' or 'as required.'
  • 不敢奉留 - We dare not keep you; '奉留' means 'to detain' or 'to keep.'
  • 恐相连累 - For fear of being implicated; '相连累' means 'to be implicated' or 'to be involved in trouble.'
  • 英遂問路出城 - Ying then asked for directions and left the city; '遂' means 'then' or 'thereupon.'
  • 陳告於平江路 - Reported to the Pingjiang Road authorities; '陳告' means 'to report' or 'to inform.'
  • 今聽侯一年 - Now waiting for a year; '聽侯' means 'to wait' or 'to be in suspense.'
  • 杳無音耗 - No news has come; '杳無音耗' means 'no news' or 'no response.'
  • 惟卖字以度日 - Surviving by selling his calligraphy; '卖字' means 'to sell calligraphy' and '度日' means 'to make a living.'
  • 不意恶札 - Unintentionally wrote a bad letter; '恶札' means 'a bad letter' or 'a poor writing.'
  • 上彻钧览 - Which reached the imperial review; '上彻' means 'to reach' and '钧览' means 'imperial review.'
  • 子既如斯 - Since you are like this; '子' is a respectful term for 'you' and '如斯' means 'like this.'
  • 付之无柰 - There is nothing to be done; '付之' means 'to leave it to' and '无柰' means 'nothing to be done.'
  • 且留我西塾 - And let you stay in my western study; '西塾' refers to a study or room in the west part of a house.
  • 训诸孙写字 - To teach my grandchildren calligraphy; '训' means 'to teach' and '诸孙' means 'grandchildren.'
  • 英幸甚 - Ying was very fortunate; '幸甚' means 'very fortunate' or 'very happy.'
  • 公延入内馆 - The official invited him into the inner hall; '延' means 'to invite' and '内馆' refers to an inner hall or room.
  • 英忽见屏间芙蓉 - Ying suddenly saw a lotus on the screen; '屏间' means 'on the screen' or 'behind the screen.'
  • 泫然垂泪 - Tears welled up in his eyes; '泫然' means 'tears welled up' and '垂泪' means 'tears fell.'
  • 何得在此 - How did it end up here; '何得' means 'how did it come to be' and '在此' means 'here.'
  • 又诵其词 - Recited the words again; '诵' means 'to recite' and '其词' means 'the words' or 'the text.'
  • 复曰 - And said; '复' means 'again' and '曰' means 'said.'
  • 何以辨识 - How do you recognize it; '何以' means 'how' and '辨识' means 'to recognize' or 'to identify.'
  • 识其字画 - Recognize the handwriting; '识' means 'to recognize' and '字画' means 'handwriting.'
  • 且其词意有在 - And the meaning of the words is clear; '且' means 'and' and '词意有在' means 'the meaning is clear.'
  • 真拙妇所作无疑 - It is undoubtedly written by a clumsy woman; '拙妇' means 'a clumsy woman' and '无疑' means 'without doubt.'
  • 若然 - If so; '若然' means 'if that is the case.'
  • 当为子任捕盗之责 - I will take on the responsibility of catching the thieves; '当' means 'will' and '捕盗之责' means 'the responsibility of catching thieves.'
  • 子姑秘之 - You should keep it secret; '姑' means 'should' and '秘之' means 'keep it secret.'
  • 乃馆英于门下 - Then invited Ying to stay under his roof; '乃' means 'then' and '门下' means 'under one's roof' or 'under one's protection.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 主翁 - Refers to the host or master, a term often used in classical Chinese to show respect.
  • 平江路 - Refers to the Pingjiang Road, an administrative division during the Yuan and Ming dynasties.
  • 听侯 - Refers to waiting for a decision or outcome, often used in legal or official contexts.
  • 恶札 - Refers to a poorly written letter or document, often with negative connotations.
  • 钧览 - Refers to the imperial review, indicating that the document or letter reached the highest level of authority.
  • 西塾 - Refers to a study or room in the west part of a house, often used for teaching or scholarly activities.
  • 诸孙 - Refers to grandchildren, a term that shows familial affection and respect.
  • 屏间芙蓉 - Refers to a lotus flower on a screen, a common decorative element in classical Chinese homes.
  • 拙妇 - Refers to a clumsy woman, often used to describe someone who is not skilled in writing or other tasks.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This long sequence must be read as a series of actions performed by the host, each introduced by a verb.
  • This sequence of clauses must be read as a series of reasons why the host sent Ying away.
  • These two clauses must be read as a sequence of actions: Ying asked for directions and left the city, then reported to the authorities.
  • This sequence of clauses must be read as a series of events: Ying waited for a year, received no news, and survived by selling his calligraphy.
  • These two clauses must be read as a sequence of events: Ying unintentionally wrote a bad letter, which reached the imperial review.
  • This sequence of clauses must be read as a series of actions and statements: Since you are like this, there is nothing to be done, and I will let you stay in my western study to teach my grandchildren calligraphy.
  • These three clauses must be read as a sequence of events: Ying was very fortunate, the official invited him into the inner hall, and Ying suddenly saw a lotus on the screen and tears welled up in his eyes.
  • These three clauses must be read as a sequence of questions and statements: How did it end up here, and Ying recited the words again, and the official said.
  • These three clauses must be read as a sequence of questions and statements: How do you recognize it, and recognize the handwriting, and the meaning of the words is clear.
  • These three clauses must be read as a sequence of statements and conditions: It is undoubtedly written by a clumsy woman, if so, I will take on the responsibility of catching the thieves, and you should keep it secret.

[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 明日，密召庆春问之 - 明日 means 'the next day,' 密召 means 'secretly summoned,' and 问之 means 'asked him.'
  • 得于何人？谁所题咏？ - 得于何人 means 'from whom did you get it?' and 谁所题咏 means 'who inscribed or composed it?'
  • 数日报云 - 数日 means 'several days later,' and 报云 means 'reported that.'
  • 同县顾阿秀舍，院尼慧圆题 - 同县 means 'from the same county,' 顾阿秀舍 means 'the residence of Gu Axiu,' 院尼 means 'nun,' and 慧圆题 means 'Huiyuan inscribed.'
  • 夫人喜诵佛经，无人作伴，闻慧圆了悟，今礼为师，愿勿却也 - 夫人 means 'the wife,' 喜诵佛经 means 'enjoys reciting Buddhist scriptures,' 无人作伴 means 'has no one to accompany her,' 闻慧圆了悟 means 'heard that Huiyuan has attained enlightenment,' 今礼为师 means 'now wishes to treat her as a teacher,' and 愿勿却也 means 'hopes you will not refuse.'
  • 院主不许 - 院主 means 'abbot' or 'head of the monastery,' and 不许 means 'refused.'
  • 而慧圆闻之，深愿一出，或者可以借此复仇，尼不能拒 - 而慧圆闻之 means 'and Huiyuan heard of it,' 深愿一出 means 'deeply wishes to leave,' 或者可以借此复仇 means 'perhaps she can use this opportunity to take revenge,' and 尼不能拒 means 'the nun could not refuse.'
  • 公命舁至，使夫人与之同寝处 - 公命舁至 means 'the official ordered her to be carried there,' and 使夫人与之同寝处 means 'and had the wife share her quarters with her.'
  • 暇日，问其家世之详 - 暇日 means 'on a leisure day,' and 问其家世之详 means 'asked about her family background in detail.'
  • 王饮泣，以实告 - 王饮泣 means 'Wang wept,' and 以实告 means 'and told the truth.'
  • 且白题芙蓉事，曰：“盗不远矣，惟夫人转以告公，脱得罪人，洗刷前耻，以下报夫君，则公之赐大矣！” - 且白题芙蓉事 means 'and mentioned the matter of the inscribed lotus,' 曰 means 'said,' 盗不远矣 means 'the thief is not far away,' 惟夫人转以告公 means 'only you can inform the official,' 脱得罪人 means 'if you can catch the culprit,' 洗刷前耻 means 'clear your past disgrace,' 以下报夫君 means 'and thus repay your husband,' and 则公之赐大矣 means 'then the official's favor will be great.'
  • 而未知其夫之故在也 - 而未知其夫之故在也 means 'but did not know that her husband's fate was already decided.'
  • 夫人以语公，且云其读书贞淑，决非小家女 - 夫人以语公 means 'the wife told the official,' 且云其读书贞淑 means 'and said that she reads books and is virtuous,' and 决非小家女 means 'definitely not a girl from a humble family.'
  • 公知为英妻无疑，属夫人善视之，略不与英言 - 公知为英妻无疑 means 'the official knew for sure that she was Ying's wife,' 属夫人善视之 means 'ordered the wife to treat her well,' and 略不与英言 means 'and barely spoke to Ying.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 尼院 - Refers to a nunnery or monastery for nuns.
  • 院尼 - Refers to a nun.
  • 佛经 - Refers to Buddhist scriptures.
  • 了悟 - Refers to spiritual enlightenment in Buddhism.
  • 题咏 - Refers to inscribing or composing poetry.
  • 芙蓉 - Refers to the lotus flower, often associated with purity and enlightenment in Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 明日，密召庆春问之 - The sentence is clear, but 密召 adds a layer of secrecy to the summoning.
  • 得于何人？谁所题咏？ - The questions are straightforward but require careful translation to maintain the interrogative tone.
  • 数日报云 - The phrase is elliptical, requiring the reader to infer the context of the report.
  • 同县顾阿秀舍，院尼慧圆题 - The sentence provides specific details about the origin of the inscription, which must be translated clearly.
  • 夫人喜诵佛经，无人作伴，闻慧圆了悟，今礼为师，愿勿却也 - The sentence is a complex request, requiring careful handling to preserve the wife's sincerity and urgency.
  • 院主不许 - The abbot's refusal is a key point, and the translation should convey his authority and decision.
  • 而慧圆闻之，深愿一出，或者可以借此复仇，尼不能拒 - The sentence describes Huiyuan's motivation and the nun's response, which must be translated to show the tension and emotional depth.
  • 公命舁至，使夫人与之同寝处 - The sentence describes the official's order and the wife's new living arrangement, which should be translated to show the official's control and the wife's compliance.
  • 暇日，问其家世之详 - The sentence describes a moment of relaxation and inquiry, which should be translated to show the wife's curiosity and the wife's willingness to share information.
  • 王饮泣，以实告 - The sentence describes Wang's emotional state and her honesty, which should be translated to show her sincerity and vulnerability.
  • 且白题芙蓉事，曰：“盗不远矣，惟夫人转以告公，脱得罪人，洗刷前耻，以下报夫君，则公之赐大矣！” - The sentence is a detailed and emotional plea, requiring careful translation to preserve the urgency and the wife's hopes.
  • 而未知其夫之故在也 - The sentence introduces a twist, and the translation should convey the wife's lack of knowledge and the tragic fate of her husband.
  • 夫人以语公，且云其读书贞淑，决非小家女 - The sentence describes the wife's description of the nun, which should be translated to show the wife's admiration and the nun's background.
  • 公知为英妻无疑，属夫人善视之，略不与英言 - The sentence describes the official's decision and actions, which should be translated to show his authority and the wife's role in the situation.

Proper Nouns

  • 庆春 - A name, likely a person involved in the story.
  • 慧圆 - A name, likely a nun.
  • 顾阿秀 - A name, likely a person whose residence is mentioned.
  • 夫人 - Refers to the wife of the official.
  • 王 - A name, likely a person involved in the story.
  • 英 - A name, likely a person involved in the story.

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又半年 - Indicates a passage of six months.
  • 進士 - Refers to a successful candidate in the imperial examinations, a scholar-official.
  • 薛理溥化 - A proper name, the full name of the official.
  • 化為 - Means 'became' or 'transformed into.'
  • 監察御史 - Refers to an official in charge of inspection and supervision.
  • 按郡 - Means 'to inspect a prefecture.'
  • 高公舊日屬吏 - Refers to a former subordinate of a high-ranking official.
  • 具語溥化 - Means 'to fully inform him.'
  • 掩捕之 - Means 'to arrest him secretly.'
  • 敕牒及家財尚在 - Means 'the imperial edict and family wealth are still there.'
  • 惟不見王氏下落 - Means 'only the whereabouts of Wang are unknown.'
  • 窮訊之 - Means 'to interrogate thoroughly.'
  • 誠欲留以配次男 - Means 'truly intended to keep her for his second son.'
  • 不復防備 - Means 'did not take precautions anymore.'
  • 不期當年八月中秋逃去 - Means 'unexpectedly escaped on the Mid-Autumn Festival of that year.'
  • 莫知所往矣 - Means 'no one knows where she went.'
  • 遂置之於極典 - Means 'placed him under the harshest punishment.'
  • 辭公赴任 - Means 'to bid farewell to the official before taking up his post.'
  • 待與足下作媒 - Means 'to wait and arrange a marriage for you.'
  • 娶而后去 - Means 'to marry first and then leave.'
  • 非晚也 - Means 'it's not too late.'
  • 糟糠之妻 - Refers to a wife who has shared poverty with her husband.
  • 同貧賤久矣 - Means 'has shared poverty for a long time.'
  • 不幸流落他方 - Means 'unfortunately has wandered to another place.'
  • 存亡未卜 - Means 'uncertain whether she is alive or dead.'
  • 且单身到彼 - Means 'and if I go there alone.'
  • 遲以岁月 - Means 'delayed by time.'
  • 萬一天地垂怜 - Means 'if heaven and earth show mercy.'
  • 若其尚在 - Means 'if she is still alive.'
  • 或冀伉俪之重谐耳 - Means 'or perhaps hope for the reunion of husband and wife.'
  • 感公恩德 - Means 'grateful for your kindness.'
  • 乃死不忘 - Means 'even death cannot forget.'
  • 别娶之言 - Means 'the words about remarrying.'
  • 非所愿也 - Means 'is not what I desire.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 進士 - Refers to a successful candidate in the imperial examinations, a scholar-official.
  • 監察御史 - Refers to an official in charge of inspection and supervision.
  • 按郡 - Refers to the inspection of a prefecture, a common administrative task.
  • 高公 - Refers to a high-ranking official, likely a former superior.
  • 薛理溥化 - A proper name, the full name of the official.
  • 王氏 - Refers to a woman, likely a concubine or妾.
  • 極典 - Refers to the harshest punishment.
  • 糟糠之妻 - A term referring to a wife who has shared poverty with her husband, often used to express loyalty and gratitude.

Proper Nouns

  • 薛理溥化 - The name of the official.
  • 高公 - A high-ranking official.
  • 王氏 - A woman, likely a concubine or妾.
  • 英 - The name of the person being addressed.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 凄然 - Term expressing a feeling of sadness or sorrow.
  • 足下高谊如此 - Polite expression meaning 'Your noble friendship is so great.'
  • 天必有以相佑 - Phrase meaning 'Heaven must surely provide some aid.'
  • 安敢苦逼 - Phrase meaning 'How dare I press you hard.'
  • 奉饯 - Term meaning 'to see off' or 'to bid farewell with a banquet.'
  • 了今生缘 - Phrase meaning 'to conclude one's earthly fate' or 'to fulfill one's earthly bond.'
  • 英故妻也 - Phrase meaning 'He is my former husband.'
  • 备道其始末 - Phrase meaning 'to recount the entire story.'
  • 慧圆则英妻改字也 - Phrase meaning 'Huiyuan is the name her former husband changed her to.'
  • 满座为之掩泣 - Phrase meaning 'Everyone in the hall was moved to tears.'
  • 盛德为不可及 - Phrase meaning 'His great virtue is beyond reach.'
  • 奴婢各一 - Phrase meaning 'one servant and one maid.'
  • 赀遣就道 - Phrase meaning 'to provide funds and send on the way.'
  • 重过吴门 - Phrase meaning 'to pass through Wu again.'
  • 薨矣 - Term meaning 'passed away' (referring to the death of a high-ranking person).
  • 夫妇号哭 - Phrase meaning 'the couple wept bitterly.'
  • 如丧其亲 - Phrase meaning 'as if they had lost a close relative.'
  • 建水陆斋三昼夜 - Phrase meaning 'to establish a water and land斋 for three days and nights.'
  • 长斋念观音不辍 - Phrase meaning 'to maintain a continuous斋 and chant the name of Guanyin.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴门 - Refers to the city of Suzhou, historically known as Wu.
  • 水陆斋 - A type of Buddhist ceremony involving both vegetarian and non-vegetarian offerings.
  • 观音 - Refers to Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of compassion in Buddhism.
  • 长斋 - Refers to a prolonged period of vegetarianism and fasting as a form of religious observance.

Proper Nouns

  • 公 - Refers to the speaker, likely a high-ranking official.
  • 崔县尉 - Refers to Cui, the county magistrate.
  • 慧圆 - Refers to Huiyuan, the speaker's former wife.
  • 英 - Refers to Ying, the speaker's former husband.
  • 吴门 - Refers to Suzhou.
  • 观音 - Refers to Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of compassion.
  • 王氏 - Refers to the family of Wang.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 真之才士陆仲甗 - Refers to a talented scholar named Lu Zhongyan, with '真之' indicating his authenticity or genuineness as a scholar.
  • 作画芙蓉屏歌 - Means 'composed a song about the painted lotus screen,' where '芙蓉屏' refers to a screen painted with lotus flowers.
  • 以纪其事 - Means 'to record the events,' indicating the purpose of the song.
  • 因录以警世云 - Means 'therefore, it is recorded to warn the world,' suggesting the song has a moral or cautionary purpose.
  • 妾忍题屏风 - Means 'I dare not inscribe on the screen,' where '妾' is a humble term used by a woman, and '忍' indicates reluctance or unwillingness.
  • 屏间血泪如花红 - Means 'the blood and tears on the screen are as red as the flowers,' a vivid metaphor for sorrow.
  • 败叶枯梢两萧索 - Means 'withered leaves and dry branches are both desolate,' describing a bleak scene.
  • 断缣遗墨俱零落 - Means 'torn silk and scattered ink are all fallen apart,' indicating the decay of art and memory.
  • 去水奔流隔死生 - Means 'the flowing water separates life and death,' symbolizing the passage of time and the inevitability of death.
  • 孤身只影成飘泊 - Means 'alone and in solitude, I become a wanderer,' expressing loneliness and displacement.
  • 残骸向谁托 - Means 'to whom can my remains be entrusted,' a poignant question about the afterlife.
  • 泉下游魂竟不归 - Means 'the soul in the spring (grave) does not return,' indicating that the dead cannot return to the living world.
  • 图中艳姿浑似昨 - Means 'the beautiful figure in the painting is still like yesterday,' suggesting that the memory of the deceased remains vivid.
  • 那禁秋雨复秋霜 - Means 'how can I endure the autumn rain and frost again,' expressing the pain of enduring hardship.
  • 宁肯江湖逐舟子 - Means 'rather than follow a boatman on the rivers and lakes,' indicating a preference for a simpler, more natural life.
  • 甘从宝地礼医王 - Means 'willingly follow the Medicine King in the sacred place,' where '医王' (Medicine King) likely refers to a revered figure in Buddhism or medicine.
  • 医王本慈悯 - Means 'the Medicine King is inherently compassionate,' emphasizing the compassionate nature of the Medicine King.
  • 慈悯怜群品 - Means 'compassionately pity all beings,' indicating the Medicine King's universal compassion.
  • 逝魄愿提撕 - Means 'the soul wishes to be pulled out,' suggesting a desire for liberation from suffering.
  • 茕嫠赖将引 - Means 'the lonely widow relies on being led,' indicating a need for guidance and support.
  • 芙蓉颜色娇 - Means 'the lotus flower is charming,' describing the beauty of the lotus.
  • 夫婿手亲描 - Means 'the husband personally painted it,' indicating the personal and intimate nature of the artwork.
  • 花萎因折蒂 - Means 'the flower withers because the stem is broken,' a metaphor for the loss of life due to external forces.
  • 干死为伤苗 - Means 'drying up is caused by injuring the seedling,' another metaphor for the loss of life due to harm.
  • 蕊干心尚苦 - Means 'the heart is still bitter even though the petals are dry,' expressing enduring pain.
  • 根朽恨难消 - Means 'the root rots and the hatred is hard to erase,' indicating deep-seated resentment.
  • 但道章台泣韩翃 - Means 'it is said that Han Hong wept at Zhangtai,' a reference to a historical or literary allusion.
  • 岂期甲帐遇文箫 - Means 'who would have expected to meet Wen Xiao in the Jia tent,' another historical or literary allusion.
  • 芙蓉良有意 - Means 'the lotus flower indeed has meaning,' suggesting that the lotus holds symbolic significance.
  • 芙蓉不可弃 - Means 'the lotus cannot be abandoned,' indicating the importance of the lotus in the context.
  • 幸得宝月再团圆 - Means 'fortunately, the moon and the pearl are reunited,' a metaphor for reunion and harmony.
  • 相亲相爱莫相捐 - Means 'love each other and do not abandon each other,' emphasizing the importance of mutual love and support.
  • 谁能听我芙蓉篇 - Means 'who can listen to my lotus flower poem,' expressing a desire for someone to understand and appreciate the poem.
  • 人间夫妇休反目 - Means 'husbands and wives in the world should not quarrel,' a moral lesson about marital harmony.
  • 看此芙蓉真可怜 - Means 'look at this lotus flower, it is truly pitiable,' expressing sympathy for the lotus and its symbolic meaning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陆仲甗 - A talented scholar, likely a historical figure or a fictional character in the context of the poem.
  • 芙蓉屏 - A screen painted with lotus flowers, often used in Chinese art and literature to symbolize purity and beauty.
  • 章台 - A place name, often associated with beauty and elegance in Chinese literature.
  • 韩翃 - A historical figure, possibly a poet or a notable person mentioned in the context.
  • 甲帐 - A tent, often associated with royalty or nobility.
  • 文箫 - A historical or literary figure, possibly a poet or a notable person mentioned in the context.
  • 宝月 - A metaphor for the moon, often used in Chinese poetry to symbolize reunion and harmony.
  • 医王 - A revered figure in Buddhism or medicine, often associated with healing and compassion.

Proper Nouns

  • 陆仲甗 - A talented scholar, likely a historical figure or a fictional character.
  • 韩翃 - A historical figure, possibly a poet or a notable person.
  • 文箫 - A historical or literary figure, possibly a poet or a notable person.
  • 医王 - A revered figure in Buddhism or medicine.

[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秋千會 - A term for a spring festival involving swings, which was a popular entertainment activity in the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 窮極富贵 - Means 'extremely wealthy and powerful,' indicating the highest level of social status.
  • 敬礼贤士 - Means 'respecting and honoring scholars,' indicating a high regard for intellectual and cultural pursuits.
  • 春色满园关不住，一枝红杏出墙来 - A famous line from a poem by Ye Shao-weng, meaning 'the spring scenery cannot be confined within the garden; a branch of red apricot flowers extends over the wall.' This alludes to the beauty and vitality of the garden.
  • 花卉之奇，亭榭之好 - Means 'the oddity of flowers and the beauty of pavilions,' emphasizing the garden's unique and attractive features.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '抵暮，生归，见诗，知为娉作，深悔一出，不得相见。' is elliptical and must be read as a sequence of events: 'Until dusk, the man returned home, saw the poem, realized it was written by Ping, and deeply regretted going out, as he could not see her.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '诗后，复写一词，名《青玉案》' introduces a new literary work after the poem, but the structure is slightly ambiguous. It could be read as 'After the poem, another piece of writing is written, titled *Qing Yu An*.'
  • The couplet '合欢花下曾相见，犹记把毫题彩扇' describes a past meeting and the act of writing on a fan. The structure is clear but requires careful translation to preserve the imagery.
  • The phrase '朝思暮想，倚门挨户，无虑千来遍' describes intense longing and waiting. The structure is somewhat elliptical, requiring interpretation to convey the full meaning.
  • The couplet '灵犀一点悬春线，残梦惊回梁上燕' uses metaphors to describe a deep connection and a disrupted dream. The structure is poetic but requires careful translation to preserve the imagery.
  • The phrase '惆怅佳期成又变' expresses the pain of fleeting happiness. The structure is clear but requires careful translation to convey the emotional weight.
